source_pr.title: table/tables: fix a DATA RACE in a unit test case
source_pr.body:

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

```
[2020-12-14T06:38:19.913Z]       /home/jenkins/agent/workspace/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/go/pkg/mod/github.com/pingcap/check@v0.0.0-20200212061837-5e12011dc712/check.go:739 +0x113
```

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

Do not call `tk2.Exec("rollback")` because it races with the operation in the goroutine.

How it Works:

Fix a data race.


### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test


### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note

symptoms: Go race detector report in TestConstraintCheckForUniqueIndex: concurrent commit/rollback paths race on the same transaction while a background goroutine performs inserts.
failure_signature: WARNING: DATA RACE between `tikvTxn.Commit()` and `tikvTxn.Rollback()` (table/tables/tables_test.go TestConstraintCheckForUniqueIndex).
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness was caused by unsafe concurrency in the unit test: it starts a goroutine that runs SQL on `tk2` while the main goroutine commits/rolls back sessions, leading to a race between commit and rollback paths on the underlying transaction. This PR fixes the data race by simplifying the synchronization/expectation logic (asserting on the channel value indicating whether `tk2` is blocked) and by removing the explicit rollback calls that raced with the goroutine's operations.
fix_pattern: Simplify synchronization (channel-based expectation) and remove racy rollback calls that can overlap with goroutine SQL execution; keep the test assertion deterministic.

evidence: evidence: PR title: table/tables: fix a DATA RACE in a unit test case
evidence: simplify channel synchronization and assertion to avoid race in concurrent insert test (table/tables/tables_test.go)
